steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces the second sentence, which describes the title snapping the viewer back to reality after the first sentence describes imagining distant planets. The transition must signal that this — the title bringing you back to Earth — is the eventual or final outcome of the viewing experience.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Consequently,’: a cause-and-effect transition implying that imagining other planets causes the title to bring you back. The title’s effect is not caused by the wandering; the two are sequential, not causal. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Alternatively,’: signals another option or possibility, as though the viewer either imagines planets or gets brought back. Both things happen in sequence; they are not alternatives. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Ultimately,’: means “in the end” or “eventually,” fitting a sequence where the initial impression gives way to a final revelation. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Additionally,’: signals agreement or addition, but the second sentence is the opposite of the first — not an addition to it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
